TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Tsou, story: None-1

S.FORM: ohye mo s'a avaho mo uh tan'e ho baito to 'o'oko?.
S.TRANSL: 你是再一次來這邊探望孩子們？
You came here again to see your children?

S.FORM: ya yupabobaito.
S.TRANSL: 是, 互相探望。
Yes, to see each other.

S.FORM: te o'a.
S.TRANSL: 沒有要...
Won't (you)...

S.FORM: mine sngʉsngʉyo XcuX n'a ahta thovaha 'o moso la feofeongo.
S.TRANSL: 有洞的地方還在鋪路嗎？
Where there were holes (in the road) is not yet paved?

S.FORM: a'ʉmtʉ 'sio.
S.TRANSL: 真是的(這些坑洞實在是.....)
It's really... (These holes are really ...)

S.FORM: hoN thovaha ho lata lata Xo'te asngʉcaX mia ta ak'i.
S.TRANSL: 爺爺沒有常常走怎麼要再鋪呢？
If Grandfather doesn't often walk on it, why it needs to be paved?

S.FORM: YM: te'o i'vaha mia 'ia mi mimu i'vaho novei to atuhcumu emoo.
S.TRANSL: 你們有再住回去(原來的)房子嗎？
Did you live back to your original house again?

S.FORM: oh te te s'a av'a 'o 'o 'ongko to ceoa to ic'o.
S.TRANSL: 那個土地的名稱要怎麼說呢？
(what) shall (we) say the name of that land?

S.FORM: zou s'a 'oahng to sngʉsngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: 是不是是Sngʉsngʉyo的一部份？
Is it a part of Sngʉsngʉyo?

S.FORM: ma asonʉ o'a.
S.TRANSL: 應該不是。
It's probably not so.

S.FORM: ohe X la eanca zou sngʉsngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: 過去一直都是這樣講是稱為sngʉsngʉyo。
(people) used to say sngʉsngʉyo.

S.FORM: sngʉsngʉyo uN.
S.TRANSL: 是Sngʉsngʉyo。
Sngʉsngʉyo (place name).

S.FORM: la la manci s'a eaca sngʉsngʉ 'a la s'a no enca 'a mo s'a man'i na sngʉyosi.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼會叫做sngʉsngʉyo? 是不是稱為... 是不是叫sngʉyo的東西很多？
How come it is called Sngʉsngʉyo? Are there ...Are there many things called Sngʉsngʉyo?

S.FORM: nia la enca sngʉyo 'o la enca sngʉsngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: (我們)通常說Sngʉsngʉyo的地方，以前稱為Snguyo嗎？
Was it called Snguyo (the place we) normally call Sngʉsngʉyo?

S.FORM: uk'a na maitan'e 'a panto nia lea.
S.TRANSL: 今天已經沒有了。以前有。
It's not now, but it was in the past.

S.FORM: zou moso la.
S.TRANSL: 是啊，以前...
Yes, in the past...

S.FORM: zou kukuzo honci o'te cuma na sngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: Sngʉyo是草的植物還是什麼呢？
Sngʉyo is the plant like straw or something?

S.FORM: evi.
S.TRANSL: 是樹。
Tree.

S.FORM: evi evi hoN alʉ kaahkʉmnʉ ci evi 'o'okosi 'o hʉngʉsi.
S.TRANSL: 是很粗的樹，竟然葉子很小。
(It is the) tree that is thick, but the leaves are small.

S.FORM: mi mio maica ko'ko isi yainca sngʉsngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: 是這樣才稱為sngʉsngʉyo。
(it is) in this way, so (people) called it Sngʉsngʉyo.

S.FORM: sngʉsngʉyo mhm ayovcu.
S.TRANSL: Sngʉsngʉyo 的植物一直延伸下去(形容從哪裡一直綿延下去)。
(The plant sngʉyo kept) extending (its habitat).

S.FORM: X to nia vungki masakici masakici.
S.TRANSL: 對，已故的莊清吉。
Yes, the Masakici who has died.

S.FORM: hoN to niae ac'ʉhʉ to ic'o na moso n'a ma man'i na sngʉyo.
S.TRANSL: 那個很多的sngʉyo是一直到那邊。
A lot of Sngʉyo extend their habitat to that place.

S.FORM: zou oh sngʉsngʉyo X sngʉsngʉyo 'a iko n'a cohivi na ina atuhcu sngʉsngʉyo la enca.
S.TRANSL: 你還知道那個真正的sngʉsngʉyo在哪裡？
Do you know where the so called Sngʉsngʉyo is?

S.FORM: lea leav'a 'e omzasi 'e sngʉsngʉyo?.
S.TRANSL: 這個Sngʉsngʉyo的上面是叫什麼？
(What is) the above of Sngʉsngʉyou usually called?

S.FORM: 'e omzasi zou 'o la enca zou c'u 'o la enca cuu?.
S.TRANSL: 這個它的上面就是我們說...就是我們說池塘？
The above of it is what (we/people usually) say 'pond'?

S.FORM: 'a i'o peohna ne to lasi eoni to na ito kesa 'o la enca cuu.
S.TRANSL: 那個稱為cuu的是Kesa(的家)下面。
Cuu locates below where Kesa lives

S.FORM: oh cuu hu ah.
S.TRANSL: Cuu...是湖啊。
Cuu... is lake.

S.FORM: ah 'ao mo i'o panto Xpanto la eaincaX hipongana.
S.TRANSL: 有一個叫做hipongana的地方。
There is a place called Hipongana.

S.FORM: hipongana o'a no eni eni 'o la enca hipongana?.
S.TRANSL: Hipongana。稱為hipongana的地方不是這裡嗎？
Hipongana. Isn't the so-called Hipongana this place?

S.FORM: zou zou 'o ihe yoni to nia noliuki na atuhcu hoci o'te na isi enca hipongana.
S.TRANSL: 主要的地方是已故的noliuki那邊，或是...那個稱為Hipongana的。
(The main area located where) the late Noliuki lived, or perhaps (the place) called Hipongana.

S.FORM: mais'a ic'o na isi atuhca eainca hipongana.
S.TRANSL: 好像是那個主要稱作hipongana的地方。
It seems to be the main area called Hipongana.

S.FORM: hoN 'ia moso la yon to ic'o.
S.TRANSL: 喔，是在那邊喔。
Oh, yes, it is there.

S.FORM: 'a oho c'o la eno enca voehipongana 'o nia amovoyu.
S.TRANSL: 那個已故的 amo voyu以前就是直接稱它voehipongana。
Amo Voyu, who died, called it Voehipongana.

S.FORM: oh voehipongana o'a eni na la enca hipongana.
S.TRANSL: Voehipngana? 稱為hipongana的不是這裡?
Voehipongana? The so-called Hipongana is not here?

S.FORM: Xo'a nte s'a enca eni hipongana te av'aX.
S.TRANSL: 這裡不是稱作hipongana嗎？要怎麼講...
Here is not called Hipongana? What should (we) say...

S.FORM: oho s'a la yaeza enca cuu eni?.
S.TRANSL: 以前這裡也稱為cuu(池塘)嗎？
Is here called Cuu?

S.FORM: c'o acʉha enca cuu 'a i'o i.
S.TRANSL: 現在都只稱為Cuu。
Now (people) in general just call it Cuu.

S.FORM: 'a i'o isi no yoni to hisayosi 'o la yaeza enca cuu.
S.TRANSL: 那個hisayosi住的地方也稱為Cuu。
(The place where) Hiayosi lives is also usually called Cuu.

S.FORM: mhm ci c'o man'i ci ongko o'te a'ʉmta asansana.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼會那麼多名字？都不太確定。
How come there ar so many names. (They are not) ascertained.

S.FORM: 'a mo no 'a mo no maica 'e maoongko ta hpʉhpʉngʉ ta ci cou mo mo 'o'okosi 'o isi 'o mo yaa maoongko.
S.TRANSL: Tsou的地方的地名都是這樣，小小地方都有名字。
The place name in Tsou area is like this. (the place which) has a name is small.

S.FORM: X 'o'okosi 'o mo yaa ongko ta tan'evo.
S.TRANSL: 這邊，小小地方都有地名。
Here, (the place which) has name is small.

S.FORM: 'a 'a mo'u 'so la yon tan'e 'a s'a na'no co'cohivi na mo c'o 'o'okosi.
S.TRANSL: 我以前不在這裡，所以我不知道那個小小的(地名)。
I didn't live her, so I don't know (the place name which) is small.

S.FORM: mhm na 'o 'a la 'a ihe n'a no seolʉa no i'vaha i'ima maitan'e 'o mo macocono.
S.TRANSL: 現在他們正在再重新尋找那一個一個(地名)。
Now they are looking for each one (place name) again.

S.FORM: mo n'a i'o isi yoni to isi yoni to nia la papay to masayosi.
S.TRANSL: 那個..那個住在以前那個masayosi的水稻田。
(The one who) used to live in the rice paddy of Masayosi.

S.FORM: te nana a'upa yainca 'o isi yoni to lotozong yabʉtnana.
S.TRANSL: 那個羅德榮住的地方是不是 也可以稱為yabʉtnana？
(Where Lotozong lived) should be included and called Yabutnana?

S.FORM: 'ia 'o mo yonne ne'esi.
S.TRANSL: 是那個在yabutnana過去的那邊。
(the place) should be there (over Yabutnana).

S.FORM: ne 'o yat'tengʉ yabʉtnana yabʉtnana ya nana yabʉtnana zou yabʉtnana.
S.TRANSL: 對啦，是Yabutnana。
Yes, Yabutnana.

S.FORM: 'a isi na'na cohivi to mh na ito.
S.TRANSL: 那個羅朝明很知道。
That Lochaoming knew this well.

S.FORM: zou to kesa.
S.TRANSL: 對 ，羅朝明。
Yes, Kesa.

S.FORM: 'a no 'so yatan'e.
S.TRANSL: 因為(他)是這裡的(人)。
Because (he is a) local (resident).

S.FORM: oh hoN ae ae aesi yatan'e.
S.TRANSL: 從一開始一直都是這邊的(人)。
(He is) always a resident here.

S.FORM: 'a zou eno.
S.TRANSL: 這樣嗎？
Is it so?

S.FORM: zou te tesi acʉha cohivi.
S.TRANSL: 對啊，他通通知道。
Yes, he knows everything.

S.FORM: sia na mo maafo ho miko uh tan'e? sia na maafo?.
S.TRANSL: 你來這裡,是誰帶你過來？
Who carried you when you came here?

S.FORM: ceoceonʉ X 'ia c'o smaina'o.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼走那麼久啊。
(how come you walked) so long.

S.FORM: i'o ake'i n'a 'unto 'unto.
S.TRANSL: 稍微運動運動。
Do a little bit exercise.

S.FORM: 'ia hoN iho mo n'a hie na miko smai'unu tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 你走過來時，天還是亮著的嗎？
When you walked here, the sky was still bright?

S.FORM: mio n'a.
S.TRANSL: 還亮著。
It is.

S.FORM: zou mo eobai' 'o nte mahafo.
S.TRANSL: 因為本來要帶我的人吵架。
(Because the people) supposed to carry (me) were arguing.

S.FORM: all: 'a mo s'a no 'so eobai ho 'auhohoi.
S.TRANSL: 是不是(因為)搶著要帶你吵架嘛！
Because (they) were arguing and competing to bring you here, aren't they?

S.FORM: la yomzeni koicʉ ta mamameoi maitan'e ho.
S.TRANSL: 今天老人家用來責備人的方式。
(These words) are usually used by seniors to blame.

S.FORM: lahin'i ehtothomʉ 'o tahin'i akoyu no maafo a'o.
S.TRANSL: 準備要帶我的人都會打鬥一番。
Those who intend to carry me usually fight (in advance for a chance to pick me).

S.FORM: YM: atavei coeconʉ 'o isi 'auhohozi meohafa.
S.TRANSL: 他們爭著要帶的人最後走路啦。
Lastly, the one who they competed to carry walked.

S.FORM: Xcu amzo sʉc'ʉhʉ mihin'iX apemzo 'ako te Xteseo'hoiX no ntehin'i maafo mhm 'ako mici.
S.TRANSL: 一直到都快走到目的地了，那些爭著要載我的人還在吵架(爭著還要帶我)。
(I) almost (walk) to (the destination), those who intended to pick me was still fighting.

S.FORM: oh mameoi ci yoneko aʉmtʉ 'a la pahicocvo hoci.
S.TRANSL: Yoneko(女的)長輩真的不會開玩笑。
Yoneko (a woman senior) cannot make a joke really.

S.FORM: 'a ne omza la no usa ho L3JsiukaiL3J ne.
S.TRANSL: 就是那時候我們去作家庭聚會(家庭禮拜)在...
It was when we went to do worship when ...

S.FORM: to moo to cumas'a kazuo.
S.TRANSL: 那個是誰的家裡，是kazuo嗎？
at whose house? Kazuo?

S.FORM: zou ne osi la eainca moso aʉmtʉ moso aʉmtʉ seolʉa no na'no mʉchʉ.
S.TRANSL: 那時候他(yoneko)講說正在下很大的雨。
At that time she (Yoneko) said it was raining cat and dog.

S.FORM: mimza cu esmi micu maezo esmi.
S.TRANSL: 我們到達，他(yoneko)也到達。
We arrived; (she) also arrived.

S.FORM: eainca manci s'a isi tuocosi to ak'i'u.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼什麼? 我的外公在問說...
Why? My grandfather was asking...

S.FORM: ia Xmanci s'aX toicu ho coeconʉ.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼走著走著到達的時候身體是濕的？
Why (you) were wet when (you) walked to here?

S.FORM: 'ama mi'o c'o mozu coeconʉ.
S.TRANSL: (yoneko 回答) 我...(怎麼樣)，乾脆走路啊。
I just walked stratightforward.

S.FORM: apemzo yobai 'o ntehin'i mahafo.
S.TRANSL: 他們只顧著吵架，原來要帶他的人(＝他們)(埋怨沒有人帶他)。
Those who planned to carry (me) were concernted on fighting.

S.FORM: micu amzo sʉc'ʉhʉ ancu mihin'i ahtu.
S.TRANSL: 人都已經到達了，他們還沒.....
(The one who walked) had arrived; they yet....

S.FORM: asonʉ 'ancu mihin'i ahtu ahtu ʉmnʉ ci sootang na tehin'i mahafo moezʉ coeconʉ cu amzo sʉc'ʉhʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他們要帶的人還沒有協調好到底誰要帶，所以就乾脆走路，都快要到了。
(the one) of them who would carry me was still negotiating. I just walked straightforward. I almost arrived (here) anyway.

S.FORM: XciX ha'o smocaefi os'o na'na ucia hafa.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼就這樣走過去，我很想帶。
Why (you) just walked by. I really wanted to carry (you).

S.FORM: maka mo 'so alʉ smaihoi ne tappang.
S.TRANSL: 竟然是從達邦走過來的。
Unexpectedly, (he) walked from Tapangu.

S.FORM: 'so alʉ covhi ho te smaihoi ne'e ho yungeou.
S.TRANSL: 從那邊(達邦)接過來的話相當遠，(竟然走路都到達了)。
It is very far if (one) welcome people from there (Tapangu). (Unexpectly he walked and arrived.)

S.FORM: zou na aʉmtʉ ci 'a mo c'o s'a maeno'u.
S.TRANSL: 是真的嗎？ 有可能是在開玩笑。
Is it true? (She) should be just joking.

S.FORM: ma memealʉ bua Xmio nana alʉX pahicocvo.
S.TRANSL: 她很擅於說...開玩笑。
(She's) good at speaking....at joking.

S.FORM: pahicocvo 'o mameoi ci yoneko.
S.TRANSL: Yoneko長輩不知道是不是開玩笑。
(It's) uncertain whether senior Yoneko was joking.

S.FORM: mhm mo na'no yoa mo 'so o o'u la talʉa moso la na'no yoayokeo.
S.TRANSL: 我記得她以前是非常健康健朗。
I remember that she used to be very healthy.

S.FORM: 'a na mo s'a ngenki maitan'e.
S.TRANSL: 她現在已經沒有很健康。
She seems not so healthy now.

S.FORM: hoN oh eh micu maezo ic'o maeo mo nongongo.
S.TRANSL: 她也...現在也是笨笨的。
(She has) also (became) delirous.

S.FORM: ah Xte c'u s'oX mameoi.
S.TRANSL: 他要老了。(指年歲要變很老了)。
(She's getting) old.

S.FORM: te c'u taeci nansai maita'e?.
S.TRANSL: 現在大概要幾歲了？
Now how old (is she)?

S.FORM: MYG: naho lasi asngʉca eainca mo congo 'o mucusi.
S.TRANSL: 他常常講他的手在痛。
She usually says her hands are aching.

S.FORM: hoN kaebʉ pasunaeno ne moso n'a la.
S.TRANSL: 當她還(年輕or健康的時候...)，她喜歡唱歌。
(She) liked to sing when she was still (young or healthy).

S.FORM: 'ana mo mai ne moso n'a la aʉmtʉ.
S.TRANSL: 已經不像她以前那樣。真的是...
It is not like what she used to be.

S.FORM: moso la na'no pasunaeno iho la XmiepiemiX.
S.TRANSL: 他以前很喜歡唱歌 ，當.....
(She) loved to sing, when....

S.FORM: mhm popeispak'i ne moso n'a la ngengki.
S.TRANSL: 他還健康的時候還很活潑，愛說笑。
When (she) was still energetic, (she) loved to make jokes.

S.FORM: zou 'ana s'a 'ana mo s'a peispak'i maitan'e mo cu la 'ana la ngenki.
S.TRANSL: 現在他不會愛玩了，已經不健康了。
Now (she) doesn't make fun; (she) is not healthy.

S.FORM: zou so ahtu ic'o 'o aʉlʉ alʉ mama to kazuo.
S.TRANSL: (Yoneko) 不是Kazuo真正的媽媽。
(Yoneko) is not the real mother of Kazuo.

S.FORM: o'a.
S.TRANSL: 不是。
No.

S.FORM: YM: 'ia 'a i'o zou 'o cumas'a.
S.TRANSL: 那個...是，是那個什麼的這樣子。
uh... it should be like this.

S.FORM: X'o isi vcongʉX to lao.
S.TRANSL: (kaneko (yoneko 的女兒) 是老(孔)的太太。
(Kanekoi, yoneko's daughter, is Lao-Khong's wife.

S.FORM: k kaneko zou kaneko zou Xic'oX 'o aʉmtʉsi XokoX mo c'o cihi.
S.TRANSL: 他(yoneko)真正的小孩只有一個。
The real her (yoneko's) child is one.

S.FORM: asonʉ maezo noacachiana zou moso nanat'ot'ohaesa 'o i'o nia singenovu.
S.TRANSL: 好像也是莊家，跟過世的singenovu是兄妹關係。
(she) probably also is one of the Noacachiana family. The past Singenove was (her) sibling.

S.FORM: iko cohivi 'o singenovu?.
S.TRANSL: 你知道 singenovu嗎？
Do you know Singenovu?

S.FORM: 'a os'o.
S.TRANSL: 我知道。
I do.

S.FORM: singenoli.
S.TRANSL: (跟)Singenoli (是兄妹)。
(She and) Singenoli (are siblings).

S.FORM: moso 'so la yone o'oi'i 'o 'a moso la yon tan'e 'o isi eainca singenoli 'o 'ohaeva to singenovu.
S.TRANSL: singenoli以前是在這裡的，他是singenovu的哥哥。
Singenoli used to live here; (he) is the elder brother of Singenovu.

S.FORM: moso nanat'ot'ohaesa to ic'o 'o nia aʉlʉ ino to kazuo.
S.TRANSL: kazuo的親媽媽跟singenoli, singenovu是兄妹。
Kazuo's real mother was sister of Singenoli and Singenovu.

S.FORM: mhm oh ohe la na'no akuzkuzoa to maya.
S.TRANSL: 以前日本人對(他們那一家人)虐待。
Japanese used to torture the family.

S.FORM: hoN ho Xasnguca tiusnaX to maya.
S.TRANSL: 日本人一直打他們。
Japanese kept beating them.

S.FORM: cohivi ta ak'i ci mita 'so maezo ea ne tapan.
S.TRANSL: 外公也知道，因為他也是達邦人。
Grandfather knows (this), because he is also from Tapangu.

S.FORM: duei 'ia.
S.TRANSL: 原來。
Unexpectedly.

S.FORM: ntohe ucia.
S.TRANSL: 原來(那些日本人)是想要對他們(怎麼樣)。
They (those Japanese) originally planed to do something (on them).

S.FORM: mio botngonʉ na mo yon tan'e eainca mo pio ci ngiaw.
S.TRANSL: 這裡有很多人嗎？ 有幾隻小貓？
Are there many people staying here? How many cats?

S.FORM: mangi ngiaw si ho hopo.
S.TRANSL: 那個床很多貓。
(There are) many people on that bed.

S.FORM: mo s'a ecvʉhʉ 'o mo coni mas'a nte yon to mo.
S.TRANSL: 有一隻並不重。好像是在作XXX。
One (cat) is not heavy. It seems to be XXX.

S.FORM: asonʉ ma'nanac'o ho micu esmi ho te c'u seolʉa no ʉmnʉ kuici mʉchʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他們來的時候好像很難過。(看起來)正要好的時候突然下大雨。
When (they) just arrived, (they) looked disappointed. When everything was getting better, it rained hard.

S.FORM: uhm enca naho o'te ahʉyʉ uh tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 可以....(你)不用過來這邊。
It's fine... (You) don't need to come here.

S.FORM: 'a mohcuhe oh mo mo yon to aiy 'o mo c'o cono emoo uh mo tuocosʉ.
S.TRANSL: 一家人在一部車裡面在詢問。
A family in a car asked.

S.FORM: 'a panto.
S.TRANSL: 有啊。
Yes, there was.

S.FORM: ia.
S.TRANSL: 有啊。
there was.

S.FORM: te 'so nizumei 'e 'a te meelʉ noteuyunu o'a te s'a.
S.TRANSL: 有啊，因為是要20人，不能在一起。
Yes. Because there were twenty people, they couldn't be together.

S.FORM: mo nac'o honci.
S.TRANSL: 那很難過,如果...
It is/was sad if...

S.FORM: 'ancu moso la aʉlʉ uh tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 他沒有真正的來過。
He didn't really come.

S.FORM: o'a te eno.
S.TRANSL: 還沒。
Not yet.

S.FORM: isi eainca te nana te nana eainca te nana iachi teoa no tesi yoni ne X cu ivaho tenva ho enca.
S.TRANSL: 他說他要在XXX(地方)找要住的地方，然後(又再打電話說)。
He said, he himself wanted to find a place to stay at XXX, and (then called me).

S.FORM: buacuma 'a mi'o s'a eelʉ.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼辦？我沒有找到。
What to do? I didn't find out (any place to stay).

S.FORM: eainca ho nteko la panto no o'te.
S.TRANSL: 如果你...
If you...

S.FORM: 'a panto la no ananao toetoeku'u.
S.TRANSL: 本來就是這樣子。
It should be in this way.

S.FORM: 'a teko no fii to tenva te'o asonʉ ahoza faeni 'o tenva to emoomu.
S.TRANSL: 你給我電話; 我可能—先給(你們)家裡的電話。
If you give me the phone, I will probably... give (you) the phone number of our house.

S.FORM: um micu ampʉmpʉ noana'o.
S.TRANSL: 然後，過了一段時間以後...
And then, after a long time...

S.FORM: mai uk'a ci mo s'a yut'ingi.
S.TRANSL: (過了一段時間以後, 他們說)好像沒有人回答。
(After a while, they say)seems no one called back.

S.FORM: os'o cu 'i'ivaha faeni 'o o'te cono faeni.
S.TRANSL: 我又給他(另一個電話) (我)之前沒有一次給他。
<X 'I gave him another phone number X> <X I didn't give him (the whole) numberX> together at that time.

S.FORM: 'a mo 'a moso tem ocu o'u cu eʉsvʉta 'o ne'e.
S.TRANSL: 我告訴(他)這邊。
I told(him) that place.

S.FORM: mhm Xcu aʉmtʉ ivaho tenva isi eainca te na 'so tena 'soX eN to zumei na te esmi na mo nʉthʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他到後來就真的打電話，他說會有十人會到達。
He really made a phone call, saying that ten people will come.

S.FORM: XtoX 'a la 'a la na'nosi no 'a mo no kakutia ho mo nizumei.
S.TRANSL: 二十人是少的？
(The situation with) twenty people is usually just few.

S.FORM: 'a la na'nosi no sanzumei uN 'o la 'o la uh tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 通常都三十人來這裡。
(people who) came here are usually thirty people.

S.FORM: la iachi pei'i.
S.TRANSL: (他們)都自己煮。
(they) usually cooked themselves.

S.FORM: Xamc'o la 'soX tu tunohopo.
S.TRANSL: 三十人用了三個床鋪吧。
(The people) should take three beds.

S.FORM: uhm zou la na'nosi no iachi pei'i 'o ic'o ho mo esmi 'o ya ne.
S.TRANSL: 當那些從...來的人到達這邊，平常大部分都是自己煮。
When (the people) from... come, they usually cook themselves.

S.FORM: Xcumas'a 'o ohkeX hioa to ic'o mav'ov'o.
S.TRANSL: 他們是幹什麼的，他們作的事情有好幾種。
What were they? They had different jobs.

S.FORM: la la la anana'o smaikikieingi ta.
S.TRANSL: 在(四處)走一走。
(they) always walk around (in this area).

S.FORM: mimimio zou hoN la asonʉ nikagecu mohnʉskʉ.
S.TRANSL: (他們)大概兩個月來一次。
(They) probably come every two months.

S.FORM: MY: 'a lasi no hafa 'o la asvʉcʉ mihe'u c'o.
S.TRANSL: 那個老師帶來專門做...那些人
(The teacher) brought some who concentrated on doing (something).

S.FORM: mhm mo na'no omosiloi ho mo fiho.
S.TRANSL: (他:看星星的老師)跟來的時候很有趣。
When (he) came along, it is very interesting.

S.FORM: ha'o biavovei to coca.
S.TRANSL: (他)就在院子裡走來走去。
(He) just walked around the yard.

S.FORM: to eaphingi ho miunu ta.
S.TRANSL: 在門旁邊朝庭院(外面)看。
(he stood) next to the door and look toward (outside).

S.FORM: 'a zou ancu no 'so la'u no 'so ahta Xc'o poalenlakuX.
S.TRANSL: 還沒，我不會讓(他們)自己聯絡。
Not yet, I never let (them) contact (by themselves).

S.FORM: kuzo kuzo kuzo 'e mo 'e mo c'o n'a mihna esmi.
S.TRANSL: (他們)剛到時情況不好。
(When they) just arrived, (their condition) was bad.

S.FORM: oh mo botngonʉ 'o alisan.
S.TRANSL: 阿里山很多人。
There were/are many people in Alishan.'

S.FORM: ton c'o c'o maita'e ha'o esmi.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣子突然來。
(They) just in this way suddenly came.

S.FORM: 'ia.
S.TRANSL: 喔，這樣子。
Oh, OK.

S.FORM: os'o eainca 'a temza 'so mounu maitan'e.
S.TRANSL: 我(跟他們)說，我們要出門。
I said, we will go out.

S.FORM: s'a peela pa.
S.TRANSL: 沒辦法....
(I) can't....

S.FORM: mhm mo i'o i'o i'o nte masu' 'a mo na'no ʉmnʉ ho te pomi.
S.TRANSL: 如果要拔草的話很好拔'(那個草很好拔)。
If (one) wants to remove weeds, (the weeds are) easily move.

S.FORM: na'no ʉmnʉ ic'o.
S.TRANSL: 那個很好。
(It is) very good.

S.FORM: honci la honciko cu la ea no moso la.
S.TRANSL: 如果說你有曾經XXX。
If you ever have XXX.

S.FORM: honci amako sʉptʉ ci av'u 'a nte c'u ake'i.
S.TRANSL: 如果有四隻狗，可以了。
If there are four dogs, it should be acceptable.

S.FORM: mhm te no te te c'u no o'te no nomso 'e mo maica ci botngonʉ cu at'ingi.
S.TRANSL: 這麼多人是可以容下，但是.....
Such many people could fit in, but....

S.FORM: panto tento to na ito to 'ino.
S.TRANSL: 但是，媽媽那邊有手電筒。
But there was mother's flashlight.

S.FORM: o'u Jiaodai to tacoca nabian zou 'a mo at'vaesi no ʉmnʉ to ceonʉ Xic'oX.
S.TRANSL: 我有交代tacoca。那邊，路很好，很方便。
I have told people at Tacoca. (That place) is good regarding the road.

S.FORM: oh la la 'so la 'so os'o eainca teai XeniX te c'o la iachia yoyoni.
S.TRANSL: 我心裡想說，作(這個房子)，只不過打算供自己住。
I was thinking that I made this house, which is was just for ourselves to live.

S.FORM: mhm 'so h'oinʉveni honci pa eon to Xto o'a isi ananava teaiX paeon ne'e.
S.TRANSL: 如果讓他們住在沒有好好作(整修)的那邊，不好意思。
(I would be ashamed if I) let them stay in the house not well decorated.

S.FORM: mo oah'ngʉ to mo ʉmnʉ 'o mo.
S.TRANSL: XXX(可能是指看星星的人)是屬於(比較)好的那一種。
XXX is the kind of good (man).

S.FORM: la'u no 'so ahtu yʉmeʉmʉ ho isi enca yupa.
S.TRANSL: 我平常不會進門，相互說...
I don't usually enter and say to each other...

S.FORM: ako moenho no mo X mhm la 'so anana'o mimio isi cocohivi 'o nia.
S.TRANSL: 因為他知道，他原本就是到處遊走的人，所以他不會一直講XXX。
Because he knows he is expectedly to walke around, he doesn't keep talking XXX.

S.FORM: mhm 'a mo acʉhʉ ancu moso s'a la inʉskʉ esmi 'e mo acʉhʉ eon tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 在這裡的這些人還沒有人來過一次。
(People are) now staying here have never been here once in the past.

S.FORM: mo aʉmtʉ mo sʉptʉ 'o la ancu mo s'a ahtu auyu ea.
S.TRANSL: 他們四個先前未曾來過。
The four people have never been here before.

S.FORM: 'a mo n'a.
S.TRANSL: 還沒有。
Not yet.

S.FORM: lea 'so maka ci 'a la s'a man'i.
S.TRANSL: XXX並不多。
XXX is not much.

S.FORM: c'o anana'o tuotuop'opʉ.
S.TRANSL: 本來就是拔草。
(He) was just pulling out weeds.

S.FORM: zou la man'i 'o lasi eemi.
S.TRANSL: 他所說的說詞很多。
His sayings were a lot. (He had many excuses.)

S.FORM: mhm 'e'ekvo'ʉ la c'o ih'unu koacuma ta ko'ko koacumai 'e 'e 'e eatafuengu.
S.TRANSL: 他這個人會這樣子，跟別人不一樣，瞧不起山上的人。
He is used to say unfriendly word, unlike other people, despising people in the mountains.

M.TRANSL: 對著別人說很不友善的話.AF

S.FORM: zou 'a la no ake'i pot'fʉngʉ ho eainca mi'o eapeisu ho mio ma .
S.TRANSL: 對，因為XXX所以他有錢，因為他有錢，所以有一點瞧不起別人。
Yes, because of XXX, (he) is rich, and then (then) he taunts people.

S.FORM: oh mav'ov'o X'o eatatiskova taX.
S.TRANSL: 有各式各樣各種族群的人。
There are varieties of people.

S.FORM: 'a te c'u no ahtu na'no tʉc'ʉhʉ no eanengesansi 'e cou a'atoana.
S.TRANSL: 我們這些鄒人還沒有辦法跟得上外面的人。
We Tsou (has not yet been capable of) competing with people from the plain.

S.FORM: 'ia to mo aʉmtʉ eainca mo uafeihi.
S.TRANSL: 可以說慢啦。
Can be said to be 'slow'.

S.FORM: 'ua 'ua mio acʉhʉ ake'i acʉhʉ hʉlʉlʉ ta ita 'a'ausna ta ho isi aiti 'e mo mai 'e lato eon ta fuengu.
S.TRANSL: 如此(這樣子)，看來有啦，是有一點對(cou)看得起，我們在山上的這些人。
If so, (he) seems to somewhat, somewhat respect Tsou people, we live in the mountains.

S.FORM: lea ahtʉ man'i 'o la i'o la mai honci eainca ake'i potfʉngʉ 'a la s'a man'i.
S.TRANSL: 像這樣子瞧不起人的並沒有那麼多。
The situateions like this to taunt people were not so many.

S.FORM: cu panno mo la uafeihi 'e cou honci a honci aiti ake'i yupa eainenu.
S.TRANSL: Cou 有比較落後的地方，如果兩者有怎樣(或許是在比的意思)....。
There are (some parts) in Tsou getting behind. If (we) see.. (If) Tsou and other people have something in between(/compete)...

S.FORM: 'a nte alʉ mai to la mais'a mo c'o potfʉngʉ c'o.
S.TRANSL: 還不至於像那種只會譏諷人家嘲笑人家的那一種。
It's unlikely to be the type who just taunts people.

S.FORM: panto la mai to moso la aʉ na moso aiti eainca yusipeohʉ to kuyai te eyoni to okeaksang na mo maica.
S.TRANSL: 看見說他一下車就說這種是給客人住的。
It was seen that (when they) get off the car, (he said that the guest(s)) will stay in the place like this?

S.FORM: hia fafatu.
S.TRANSL: 這麼多石頭。
So many stones.

S.FORM: ma mo no 'so pepe 'o kucu.
S.TRANSL: 那鞋子很高。
The shoes were high.

S.FORM: cu 'so aʉlʉ mo 'so maica 'o vco 'o i'o hahocʉngʉ enca aʉmtʉ te mainenu ci yoni no okeksang c'o fafatu.
S.TRANSL: 只有石頭要怎麼讓客人住？
How come let guests stay (in a place) only with stone?

S.FORM: c'o osni yut'ingi 'o vcongusi a(ʉ)lʉ au 'o e'ehe yupa yupa nia no mʉ'ho 'o e'ehe.
S.TRANSL: 他太太馬上答腔，竟然很合，他們的話互相配合。
His wife answered at once. Unexpectedly, their words really met to each other's (words).

S.FORM: moso uh tan'e?.
S.TRANSL: 他們有來嗎？
(Did) they come here?

S.FORM: moso no ahtu 'a zou.
S.TRANSL: 沒有像原先預期的打算那樣過來。
(They) didn't (do) as expected.

S.FORM: 'a 'a i'o mo maica mo 'ote 'a zou.
S.TRANSL: 就沒有來。
(They just) didn't (come).

S.FORM: i'o mo maica la'u no 'aha 'a la'u s'a ahta eainca 'a la pa'teni no hia.
S.TRANSL: 像這種情況我不會... 呈現給人家看。
In such a case, I usually don't... show (people) how...

S.FORM: ci 'a la'u no umemeala n'a ake'a teai na'a ho.
S.TRANSL: 我平常不會說... (我)會稍微先處理一下狀況。
I usually don't... (I usually) carfully take care of the situation first.

S.FORM: la'u eainca temu iachia aiti hocimu ʉmnʉa 'a temu no.
S.TRANSL: 我說，你們自己看嘛！如果你們喜歡，你們就...
I said, you see on yourselves. If you like it, then.....

S.FORM: honcimu o'te ʉmnʉa temza no ahʉya eainca teko yon tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 如果你們不喜歡，我們不會說一定要你們住這裡。
If you don't like, we wouldn't definitiely want you to stay here.

S.FORM: ahʉya pamiskia um 'ia 'a mo no maica .
S.TRANSL: 一定要你們在這裡過夜...這樣子。
(We) definitely forced you to stay over night... like this.

S.FORM: te pa iachia pa iachia paketeineni na tehe hia zou.
S.TRANSL: 就是讓他們自己決定。
to et (them) make a decision by themselves.

S.FORM: eh eainca eʉsvʉta na tehe XʉmnʉaX?.
S.TRANSL: 就是說，讓他們講他們喜歡的。
Namely, let them tell what they liked.

S.FORM: teko s'o ha'o o'te miski maitan'e ak'i ho teko miko n.
S.TRANSL: (跟ak'i講)你不要隨便說今天在這裡不要過夜喔。
Grandfather, don't rashly (decide that you'll) stay over night here.

S.FORM: 'a tʉtpʉta.
S.TRANSL: (如果這樣)要把你抓起來(過夜)。
(If so), (we'll) catch (you).

S.FORM: tʉtpʉta teko tiv'oha?.
S.TRANSL: 抓，抓得住嗎？
Catch...(can) you catch hold of (him)?

S.FORM: la'u mimio ho la'u la'u ha hm la'u ah poaeisvʉsvʉtʉ.
S.TRANSL: 當我平常帶我外公的時候，我(喜歡)走來走去，我讓他講述事情。
When I (am with my grandfather), I (like to) walk around, and let (him) tell stories.

S.FORM: zou mon'i na mon'i na la cocohivi la mia ci ceonʉ.
S.TRANSL: (有關)他知道(的事)....他以前走過的路。
(about) what (he) has known... the road he walked.

S.FORM: ci hpʉhpʉngʉ afu'u o'a oho la oho la taceo'a.
S.TRANSL: 既然以前已經沒有給他砌石頭...
Since it was not built by laying stones....

S.FORM: moko moyovcʉ?.
S.TRANSL: 你有往下走嗎？
Did you go downward?

S.FORM: mosu mosu la fiho to moso la ehtothomʉ?.
S.TRANSL: 你以前曾經跟著去打仗嗎？
Did you also go to the war?

S.FORM: heetai?.
S.TRANSL: 當兵？
As a solider?

S.FORM: mo'u lea.
S.TRANSL: 我曾經有。
I did.

S.FORM: maaya mosu la eno Xu eno ko'ko la'u nana kaebaX talʉi ho lata.
S.TRANSL: 日本時代你有嗎？ 所以說我很喜歡聽他...
In the Japanese colonial period? That's why I like to hear him....

S.FORM: tea uh 'a ma mo n'a pio na mo n'a eayo to moso la fiho uh ne'e.
S.TRANSL: 那時後跟過去那邊的還有幾個人活著。
Among (the people) followed and went there, some of them are still alive.

S.FORM: micu c'o asonʉ na'no kakutia na mo n'a eayo.
S.TRANSL: 還活著的人可能已經很少了。
(People who are) still alive are probably just few.

S.FORM: na moso oh mosu s'a ohpopsohʉ?.
S.TRANSL: 你有去兩次。
You went there twice.

S.FORM: ma 'ia.
S.TRANSL: 對。
Yes.

S.FORM: 'a mohnʉskʉ.
S.TRANSL: (他)去一次。
(He) went (there) once.

S.FORM: pocenga enca honcita aʉmtʉ o'te meelʉ yuovei.
S.TRANSL: 看看是不是真的不能回來。
(I) just saw if (I) could really return.

S.FORM: 'a moso aʉmtʉ man'i 'o moso o'te meelʉ yuovei.
S.TRANSL: 沒有辦法回來的人真的很多。
(People who) couldn't return were really a lot.
